title: Write whole numbers as fractions
description: Represent fractions as whole numbers and whole numbers as fractions.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each rectangle is $1$ whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expressions describe the shaded part of the picture?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $2$ whole rectangles shaded.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each rectangle has $2$ equal parts called halves.

[[☃ image 2]]

There are $4$ halves shaded.
We can write $4$ halves as $\dfrac{4}{2}$.

These expressions describe the shaded part of the picture.

 - Four halves
 - $\dfrac{4}{2}$

options.alt: Two rectangles that are equal in size. Each rectangle is divided into 2 equal parts. Both rectangles have all their parts shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each circle is equal to $1$ whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expressions describe the shaded part of the picture?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There is $1$ whole circle shaded.

[[☃ image 1]]

The circle has $3$ equal parts called thirds.

[[☃ image 1]]

There are $3$ thirds shaded.
We can write $3$ thirds as $\dfrac{3}{3}$, or $1$ whole.

This can be represented as follows:

 -  $1$ whole
 - $3$ thirds

options.alt: A circle divided into three equal parts. All three parts are shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each rectangle is $1$ whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



**Which expressions describe the shaded part of the picture?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There is $1$ whole rectangle shaded.


[[☃ image 1]]

The rectangle has $2$ equal parts called halves.

[[☃ image 1]]

There are $2$ halves shaded.
We can write $2$ halves as $\dfrac{2}{2}$, or $1$ whole.

These expressions describe the shaded part of the picture.

 - $1$ whole
 - $\dfrac{2}{2}$

options.alt: A rectangle divided into two equal parts. Both parts are shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each rectangle is $1$ whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expressions describe the shaded part of the picture?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $2$ whole rectangles shaded.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each rectangle has $6$ equal parts called sixths.

[[☃ image 1]]

There are $12$ sixths shaded.
We can write $12$ sixths as $\dfrac{12}{6}$, or $2$ wholes.

These expressions describe the shaded part of the picture.

 - $12$ sixths

- $2$ wholes

options.alt: Two rectangles are equal in size. Each rectangle is divided into six equal parts. Both rectangles have all their parts shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each square is $1$ whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expressions describe the shaded part of the picture?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $2$ whole squares shaded.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each square has $3$ equal parts called thirds.

[[☃ image 1]]

There are $6$ thirds shaded.
We can write $6$ thirds as $\dfrac{6}{3}$, or $2$ wholes.

These expressions describe the shaded part of the picture.

 - $2$ wholes

- $\dfrac63$

options.alt: Two squares that are equal in size. Each square is divided into three equal parts. All parts of both squares are shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each circle is equal to $1$ whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expressions describe the shaded part of the picture?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:

[[☃ image 1]]

Each circle has $4$ equal parts called fourths.

[[☃ image 1]]

There are $8$ fourths shaded.
We can write $8$ fourths as $\dfrac{8}{4}$, or $2$ wholes.

These expressions describe the shaded part of the picture.


 -  $8$ fourths
 - $\dfrac84$

options.alt: Two circles that are equal in size. Each circle is divided into four equal parts. All parts of both circles are shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each circle is equal to $1$ whole.



[[☃ image 1]]

**Which expressions describe the shaded part of the picture?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There is $1$ whole circle shaded.

[[☃ image 1]]

The circle has $8$ equal parts called eighths.

[[☃ image 1]]

There are $8$ eighths shaded.
We can write $8$ eighths as $\dfrac{8}{8}$, or $1$ whole.

These expressions describe the shaded part of the picture.


 - $\dfrac88$ 

- $8$ eighths

options.alt: A circle divided into eight equal parts. All eight parts are shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each large square is $1$ whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What fraction describes the $3$ wholes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The $\maroonD{\text{numerator}}$ is the number of equal parts being counted.

The $\blueD{\text{denominator}}$ is the number of equal parts in one whole. 

$\dfrac{\maroonD{\text{numerator}}}{\blueD{\text{denominator}}}$
Each whole has $\blueD{3}$ equal parts, and there are $\maroonD{9}$ shaded parts.
The fraction that represents the $3$ wholes is $\dfrac{\maroonD{9}}{\blueD{3}}$.

options.alt: Three rectangles that are equal in size. Each rectangle is divided into three parts. All rectangles have all their parts shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
The circle is $1$ whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What fraction describes the whole?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:

The whole has $\blueD{2}$ equal parts, and there are $\maroonD{2}$ shaded parts.
The fraction that represents the whole is $\dfrac{\maroonD{2}}{\blueD{2}}$.

options.alt: A circle divided into two equal parts. Both parts are shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which fraction is equivalent to $5?$**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The $\maroonD{\text{numerator}}$ is the number of equal parts being counted.

The $\blueD{\text{denominator}}$ is the number of equal parts in one whole. 

$\dfrac{\maroonD{\text{numerator}}}{\blueD{\text{denominator}}}$

$\dfrac{\maroonD{10}}{\blueD{2}}$ means we count $\maroonD{10}$ parts with $\blueD{2}$ equal parts per whole:

[[☃ image 1]]

There are $5$ whole circles.
$\dfrac{\maroonD{10}}{\blueD{2}}$ is equivalent to $5$.
